---
title: "Solar Neutrino Mini-Project"
author: "Team name"
date: today
bibliography: references.bib
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Physics question

State the concrete question your team answered.

# Model

Write the minimum equations used in the project. Explain which parts are physical inputs and which parts are toy approximations.

# Input data

List the data files and reference values used:

- solar flux table;
- \(^{8}\mathrm{B}\)-spectrum;
- electron density profile;
- detector response parameters.

# Code and numerical method

Describe the notebook and the key functions from `src/solar_neutrino`.

Include one short code fragment only if it clarifies the method.

# Results

Insert the main figure and the numerical result:

```text
best-fit normalization =
best-fit effective Pee =
confidence interval =
```

# Discussion of approximations

State explicitly:

- fluxes are inputs, not a solved solar model;
- \(^{8}\mathrm{B}\)-spectrum may be a toy beta-like shape;
- detector response is schematic;
- backgrounds and systematics are simplified or absent.

# Conclusion

Write a short scientific conclusion that follows from the plotted result.

# References

::: {#refs}
:::
